Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Dismissal As To Defendants Fairmont Hotel Company- San Francisco .P. And Fairmont Hotel Management L.P. Only; Filed Concurrently With Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration Of
Off Calendar Nov-30-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ELLEN LEVIN, TEL. NO. 1-818-491-3410 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CHARMAINE YU OF THE COBLENTZ PATCH DUFFY & BASS LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-772-5729 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF JAMES BENN'S Motion To Set Aside Dismissal As To Defendants Fairmont Hotel Company- San Francisco L.P. And Fairmont Hotel Management L.P. Only - Motion to set aside dismissal is denied. Plaintiff's counsel has failed to demonstrate that Plaintiff is entitled to discretionary relief pursuant to CCP section 473(b). Plaintiff's reliance upon Zamaora v. Clayborn Contracting Grp., Inc. (2002) 28 Cal.4th 249 is unavailing. Plaintiff's counsel's error, in the case at hand, appears to extend beyond excusable neglect based on a clerical mistake. Plaintiff's counsel knew that she had made errors in the past in identifying the correct defendants in Plaintiff's requests for dismissal. (Levin Decl. page 3.) Rather than ensure that the defendants were accurately identified, Plaintiff's counsel instructed her associate to sign the papers submitted by Defendant's counsel without even reviewing the papers. (Plaintiff's Reply page 4.) In alleging that Defendants' counsel took advantage of Plaintiff in submitting the September 14, 2015, request for dismissal, Plaintiff does not address the fact that Defendants received notice of the April 30, 2015, dismissal request, yet were never told that the request mistakenly included all Defendants. Order signed in open court.
Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg
